headline: US-Israeli strikes begin across 24 Iranian provinces
detail: Coordinated first-strike campaign targeting military infrastructure across all 24 provinces of Iran. War begins.

headline: Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in strike
detail: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei confirmed killed. Succession crisis triggered. Mojtaba Khamenei later named successor.

headline: Minab school struck — children killed
detail: School in Minab hit during military operations. Death toll rose from initial reports to 175 children. US Army later acknowledged proximity to IRGC facility. Investigation ongoing.
casualty_source: Iranian Red Crescent / US Army acknowledgment

headline: Iran issues Strait of Hormuz closure warnings
detail: IRGC Navy begins threatening closure of Strait of Hormuz. Later formally declared closed (selective enforcement — Chinese ships passing).

headline: 6 US service members killed at Shuaiba, Kuwait
detail: First US combat deaths. 6 KIA at Shuaiba base in Kuwait from IRGC missile/drone attack. 10 seriously wounded.

headline: Iran contacts CIA via back-channel
detail: MOIS makes contact with CIA. Later revealed as Larijani back-channel via Oman.

headline: Hezbollah enters war — Lebanon front active
detail: Hezbollah opens fire from Lebanon. Attacks on Maroun al-Ras, Kfar Kila, Golan Heights, Haifa port. IDF responds with Dahiya strikes.

headline: UK grants basing + first kinetic engagement
detail: UK confirms military basing for operations against Iran. First British kinetic engagement in the conflict.

headline: Gandhi Hospital Tehran struck by Israeli strike
detail: Gandhi Hospital in Tehran hit. Neonatal ward later confirmed destroyed.

headline: Austin TX shooting — 3 dead
detail: Shooting in Austin, Texas. FBI investigating potential connection to conflict. Suspect named as Diagne.

headline: QatarEnergy halts LNG shipments
detail: QatarEnergy suspends LNG exports through Strait of Hormuz. Force majeure expected across Gulf producers.

headline: IRGC formally declares Hormuz closed
detail: IRGC formally announces closure of Strait of Hormuz. Selective enforcement: Chinese vessels observed transiting. Down to 2 transits/day from normal 50+.

headline: Natanz nuclear site struck again — 2nd attack
detail: Second strike on Natanz nuclear facility. IAEA later confirms damage. Nuclear-specific damage not fully confirmed.

headline: White House confirms 49 senior Iranian leaders killed
detail: White House announces 49 senior Iranian military and political leaders killed since start of operations. 7 confirmed by Iranian media on Day 1.

headline: US Embassy Riyadh struck by drones
detail: IRGC drones strike US Embassy compound in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.

headline: E3 (UK, France, Germany) enters war
detail: European E3 declares readiness to 'destroy' Iran capabilities. Macron delivers speech from nuclear submarine. France deploys carrier.

headline: CENTCOM destroys IRGC command & control network
detail: CENTCOM announces destruction of IRGC C2 infrastructure. IDF conducts simultaneous strikes on Tehran and Beirut.

headline: Secret nuclear facility destroyed
detail: Previously unknown nuclear facility destroyed. Parchin struck twice. Nuclear-specific damage assessment ongoing.

headline: Assembly of Experts attacked during succession vote
detail: Strike on Assembly of Experts during session to vote on Khamenei succession. Mojtaba Khamenei named successor despite attack.

headline: US submarine sinks IRIS Dena — first torpedo kill since WWII
detail: US submarine torpedoes and sinks Iranian frigate IRIS Dena off Sri Lanka. First US submarine torpedo kill since World War II.

headline: NATO intercepts Iranian missile heading toward Turkey
detail: NATO air defenses intercept Iranian ballistic missile on trajectory toward Turkey. EU later condemns attacks on Turkey and Azerbaijan specifically.

headline: F-35 scores first air-to-air kill
detail: US F-35 achieves first confirmed air-to-air kill in combat. Also: 3 F-15E lost to friendly fire (separate incident).

headline: Senate war powers resolution fails 47-53
detail: US Senate vote on war powers resolution to constrain Iran operations fails 47-53. House war powers later also defeated.

headline: IRGC drones strike Azerbaijan
detail: Iranian drones strike targets in Azerbaijan. Azerbaijan halts cross-border traffic, prepares retaliatory measures.

headline: Azadi Stadium destroyed
detail: Tehran's Azadi Stadium, Iran's largest sports venue, destroyed in strikes.

headline: BAPCO refinery in Bahrain hit — fire contained
detail: Bahrain Petroleum Company (BAPCO) refinery struck by IRGC drones/missiles. Fire eventually contained. Bahrain total: 221 projectiles intercepted.

headline: Trump demands role in choosing Iran's next leader
detail: Trump states 'I have to be involved' in selecting Iran's next leader. Called Mojtaba 'a lightweight.' Later: 'Maybe' to religious leader IF pro-US/Israel.

headline: B-2 strikes underground sites; Khamenei bunker destroyed
detail: B-2 bombers deploy penetrator munitions on deep underground facilities including Khamenei's bunker. Phase 2 officially begins. 'Space Command equivalent' facility also targeted.

headline: Iran regular Army enters combat
detail: Iran's conventional Army (Artesh) formally enters combat alongside IRGC. Will 'expand attacks in coming days.' 610,000 active forces (350K army + 190K IRGC).

headline: Trump demands 'UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER' on Truth Social
detail: Trump posts demand for unconditional surrender. Axios clarifies: means total military destruction, not formal capitulation. Leavitt announces 4-6 week timeline — first official estimate.

headline: 25 Iranian warships struck; navy 'destroyed' — Trump/CNN
detail: Trump claims via CNN that 25 Iranian warships have been struck. IRIS Shahid Bagheri burning (sinking unconfirmed). Iran retaliates by striking 'US-owned' oil tanker off Kuwait.

headline: Haaretz: Russia providing Iran intelligence on US forces
detail: Haaretz reports Russia sharing intelligence with Iran on US force positions. If confirmed: near co-belligerency. Kremlin positioning as energy beneficiary.

headline: Kurdish leaders confirm US/Israel coordination — ground op 'within week'
detail: Kurdish leaders confirm to Reuters/Al Jazeera direct coordination with US and Israel for over a year. 5,000-6,000 fighters on Iraqi border. Ground operation 'very likely within a week.' Reza Pahlavi named as opposition candidate.

headline: Iran strikes oil tanker off Kuwait — commercial shipping warfare escalation
detail: Iran strikes 'US-owned' oil tanker off Kuwait coast. Tanker on fire. Represents new escalation into commercial shipping warfare.

headline: Mehrabad airbase (Tehran) struck overnight
detail: Mehrabad airbase in Tehran hit by coalition strikes. Damage assessment pending.

headline: IDF issues displacement order for Qom — first inside Iran
detail: IDF issues civilian displacement order for the city of Qom. First such order issued for a location inside Iran proper.

headline: Tehran elementary school (#4) struck
detail: Fourth school hit in the conflict. Tehran elementary school struck. Previous schools: Minab, Parand (x2).

headline: WTI hits $92; Qatar warns $150 in 2-3 weeks
detail: WTI crude spikes to $92 (from ~$79-81 pre-war). Brent at $87. Qatar warns of $150/bbl in 2-3 weeks if Hormuz stays closed.

headline: USS Gerald R. Ford enters Red Sea via Suez
detail: USS Gerald R. Ford carrier strike group transits Suez Canal into Red Sea.

headline: Baghdad Airport area struck by Iranian missiles
detail: Iranian missiles hit Baghdad Airport area. Iraq PM states will not allow territory used as attack platform. Total Iraq dead: 13.

headline: Golestan Palace (UNESCO World Heritage) hit
detail: Golestan Palace in Tehran, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, damaged in strikes. Tel Aviv Bauhaus heritage also damaged.

headline: IDF Dahiya bombardment — 26 strikes, 123+ dead in Lebanon
detail: IDF conducts 26 strikes on Dahiya district of Beirut. IRGC Air Force command and Hezbollah naval HQ destroyed. Lebanon: 217 dead total, 798 wounded, 95,000 displaced. 700,000 affected by Beirut evacuation order.

headline: Jordan actively shooting down Iranian drones — new defensive belligerent
detail: Jordan begins actively intercepting Iranian drones over Irbid. Becomes new defensive belligerent in conflict.

headline: Maersk suspends all Gulf bookings; European naval coalition forms
detail: Maersk suspends Persian Gulf bookings. P&I insurance cancelled for Gulf region. European naval coalition (UK, France, Greece, Italy, Portugal, Spain) forms.

headline: Iranian Red Crescent: 1,332 dead, 6,000+ wounded
detail: Running totals per Iranian Red Crescent: 1,332 dead, 6,000+ wounded. UNICEF: 181 children killed. 3,090 residential + 528 commercial units destroyed. 13 medical facilities hit (WHO). Internet blackout 144+ hours.
